heading: Transportation of persons acting as private express
label: 1697 Transportation of persons acting as private express
text: Whoever, having charge or control of any conveyance operating by land, air, or water, knowingly conveys or knowingly permits the conveyance of any person acting or employed as a private express for the conveyance of letters or packets, and actually in possession of the same for the purpose of conveying them contrary to law, shall be fined under this title.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 18, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 305 (, ).Mar. 4, 1909, ch. 321, § 18235 Stat. 1124Same changes were made as in .section 1694 of this title
Amendments1994— substituted “fined under this title” for “fined not more than $150”.Pub. L. 103–322
